_comment: AC-6 (epic #416) fixtures for the deterministic AC-coverage eval. Each scenario is a spec + trace + task + config triple materialized into a temp root by scripts/run-ac-coverage-evals.mjs. `specAcs` renders `- [ ] AC{id}: {text}` lines under `## Acceptance criteria` (empty renders a spec with no AC section). `completedAcIds` seeds `impl-AC{n}` step_complete trace events. `expected` is the correct verdict derived from first principles; the harness runs the REAL AC-1 read + AC-2 pr-ready gate and the scorer asserts observed == expected. `category`: correct (a compliant task), miss (a real gap Phase 1 must catch), known-limitation (a real gap Phase 1 cannot catch — documented, not silently passed).

scenarios.note: Every approved AC has a recorded completion — the gate must not obstruct a compliant task in any mode.
AC3 never implemented. block refuses; warn records one violation but allows; off allows (pre-gate behaviour).
Agent implemented AC1 only and omitted AC2 + AC3 completions entirely — the disk read, not the agent's claim, decides.
Agent claimed AC4 (out of range — no such AC) while leaving the real AC3 undone. The bogus id is ignored (cannot inflate coverage) and the real miss is caught.
A feature spec that parses to zero ACs (malformed/absent heading) must fail closed, never pass vacuously — block refuses, warn records the zero-AC violation.
A chore lane carries no acceptance-coverage expectation — zero ACs pass in every mode with no violation.
Spec accidentally repeats AC2. computeAcCoverage de-duplicates by id (first wins), so total is 3 and full coverage passes — a numbering slip is tolerated, not thrown.

Pre-#421 an agent reported task-local AC numbers; task B's local AC1 is really global AC3, so global AC3 has no completion. #421 fixes the root cause at dispatch; this gate is the backstop that still catches the resulting miss.

Spec was revised and ACs renumbered, but impl-AC3 records a completion tied to the PRE-revision content. Phase 1 matches by id only, so this reads as fully covered and block allows. This is a documented limitation Phase 2 (commit + spec-digest binding) closes — surfaced in knownLimitations, never a silent pass.
